channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: Caroline Mulroney resigning from Ford’s cabinet, will step down as MPP next month
Wave of rejection letters sent to Ontario gov’t workers who asked to work from home as unions seek accommodations during World Cup
item.description: Unions representing Ontario Government workers say a wave of rejection letters have gone out recently to people who requested alternate work arrangements, even as the unions requests a general accommodation for employees during the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Appeal of Ontario Liberal candidate nomination in Scarborough Southwest dismissed, Ahsanul Hafiz declared ‘true winner’
item.description: An independent arbitration committee has dismissed the appeal of an Ontario Liberal candidate nomination in Scarborough Southwest.

item.title: Premier Doug Ford slams judge’s ruling on Ontario encampment
item.description: An Ontario judge’s recent decision barring the Region of Waterloo from removing people living in a homeless encampment is “cockamamie,” Premier Doug Ford said on Friday.

item.title: ‘We don’t have room’: Tires again piling up around Ontario as critics call for changes to recycling rules
item.description: Ontario’s tire recycling system appears to be under pressure, with complaints of tires piling up at dealers, mechanics and other collection sites for weeks.

item.title: Toronto island airport expansion will cost up to $5 billion, port authority says
item.description: The Toronto Port Authority says the proposed island airport expansion will cost up to $5 billion.

item.title: Ontario to ban use of Chinese-made drones for ‘highly sensitive’ police operations
item.description: The Ontario government says that it will ban the use of Chinese-made drones for “highly sensitive” policing operations amid concerns about data security.

item.title: Toronto island airport expansion would lead to severe traffic, committee hears
item.description: Toronto’s waterfront secretariat says the province’s plans to redevelop Ontario Place and expand the airport on the Toronto Islands will create severe traffic and transit congestion and could lead to degradation of the water quality in the harbour.

item.title: Ontario now testing 10 asymptomatic people for hantavirus; low-risk cases to stop isolating if negative
item.description: Ontario’s ministry of health says it is now testing 10 asymptomatic people with connections to a hantavirus-stricken cruise, a shift from government remarks made earlier this week.

item.title: Liberal MP Nate Erskine-Smith seeks advice on next steps following nomination loss
item.description: Liberal MP Nate Erskine-Smith is asking supporters for advice on what his next steps should be, with his potential bid to become Ontario Liberal leader in doubt following a nomination loss.

item.title: As clock ticks down for Ontario consumption sites, users fear they won't survive
item.description: For Riley Bisson, the unassuming one-storey house with a red brick exterior in Toronto’s downtown core is more than just a place to safely use drugs without worrying about dying of an overdose.

item.title: Ontario's more restrictive transparency law nixes request for health minister records
item.description: Ontario’s new and more restrictive freedom-of-information laws have quashed another request, this time for documents that may have given a glimpse into potential hospital cuts.

item.title: Ontario to expand jail capacity by 2,500 beds over 10 years at a cost of $3 billion
item.description: Ontario’s solicitor general says the province will expand its jails by 2,500 beds over the next 10 years at a cost of $3 billion to the taxpayers.

item.title: Ontario education minister says he can't interfere in Catholic board's Pride flag ban
item.description: Education Minister Paul Calandra has denied a request to overturn a Catholic school board’s decision not to fly the Pride flag because he cannot interfere in denominational issues, even in boards under supervision.

item.title: Here’s how much Ontario taxpayers are on the hook for in private jet purchase and sale
item.description: Ontario taxpayers are on the hook for nearly $200,000 over Premier Doug Ford’s flip-flop on a $28.9-million private jet purchase, newly released documents from the premier’s office show.
